title: beta maritima subsp. atriplicifolia
intro: Beta maritima subsp. atriplicifolia, also known as sea purslane, is a succulent plant that belongs to the family Aizoaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of sea purslane include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant protection and potentially lowering blood pressure. Sea purslane can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
